programme.title: Air Crash Investigation Special Report 2
programme.desc: Even the most experienced pilots are affected by spatial orientation disorders.This is one of the most common causes of accidents.Events related to the death of former president John F. Kennedy's son will be recalled.He died on July 16, 1999 in an accident of the plane he was piloting.Together with his wife and sister-in-law, he wanted to reach an island in Massachusetts.The captain of the jet lost his course over the Red Sea.The machine crashed.A Boeing 737 also fell to the bottom of the ocean. Its crew was unable to cope with a powerful storm off the coast of Indonesia.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of air accidents in recent years.Each episode will present a reconstruction of the events that led to the tragedy.The stories are supplemented by reports from experts and eyewitnesses.

programme.title: Drain the Oceans
programme.desc: The sea depths hide many secrets.The latest technologies will allow you to show wrecks, sunken treasures, cities and even surprising geological formations.One of the puzzles is the sinking of the unfinished aircraft carrier Graf Zeppelin.Scientists will also embark on a journey across the Pacific Ocean to explore the Pacific Ring of Fire - a region of frequent earthquakes and volcanic eruptions.In addition, specialists will analyze the history of wars, piracy and slavery in the Gulf of Mexico region and the collapse of the largest ancient civilizations in the Mediterranean basin. In 2006, Polish hydrologists, using multi-beam echosounder, discovered a wreck almost 260 meters long at the bottom of the Baltic Sea.It turned out that in the depths lies the "Graf Zeppelin" - an unfinished aircraft carrier of the Third Reich that never took part in combat.The huge unit could accommodate over 40 fighters, bombers and torpedo planes.The planes were to take off using a catapult.The ship was sunk in the Oder.The question is why it was found over 300 km from the coast, almost 90 m below sea level.

credits.actor: Self - Narrator: National Geographic Channel - Russell Boulter
Self - Norwegian University of Science and Technology - Fredrik Soreide
Self - Author and Historian - Craig L. Symonds

programme.title: The Infinite Explorer with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Professor Hannah Fry sets off on a journey around the world.He will visit Spain, Vietnam, Greece, South Korea, Ireland and Iceland.You will admire stunning landscapes and bustling cities.

programme.title: Inside Airport Lost \u0026 Found
programme.sub-title: A lost family treasure and a lost eye
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the work of the lost and found office.Search teams make every effort to return the most surprising lost items to travelers.These include artificial eyes, luxury watches and ritual robes.

programme.title: Toronto Airport Uncovered
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the operation of the largest airport in Canada.Viewers will see how the staff ensures traffic flows during record snowfall and negative temperatures.

programme.title: Frontlines
programme.desc: Various terrains and conditions in which soldiers had to fight on the fronts of World War II: from North Africa to northern Europe, from the Mediterranean Sea to the Pacific.

programme.title: To Catch A Smuggler 5
programme.desc: German law enforcement officers cooperate with HSI and CBP agents in Philadelphia.Their common goal is to catch the recipient of 30,000 ecstasy pills worth half a million dollars.A novice customs officer on duty at the southern border of the country comes across a shipment of methamphetamine for the first time.In New York, CBP seizes illegal food products that pose a potential threat to American agriculture and discovers an ancient figurine of a Hindu goddess smuggled through Hong Kong.

programme.title: Border Protection Spain
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the work of units guarding the Spanish coast.Highly trained professionals have modern equipment.They fight drug smuggling and illegal emigration.

credits.actor: Self - Biologist at Palma Aquarium Foundation - Xisca

programme.title: Genghis Khan: the Secret History of the Mongols
programme.sub-title: Temujin: The boy who became khan
programme.desc: A profile of a man who went down in history as a brilliant military strategist.The six-episode series will present little-known facts from the life of the fearless leader.The authors will show how, after a difficult childhood and captivity, he managed to gain power, unite the quarreling tribes, build an invincible army and subjugate all those who opposed him, including his former allies.Impressive conquests will be discussed, including:expansion into the West, campaigns against the neighboring Tangut people and the Chinese Jin Empire.The troops he commanded were known for the mobility of their cavalry, carrying out rapid attacks and retreats, and applying psychological pressure on their opponents.They used innovative techniques when besieging even well-fortified cities.Genghis Khan became one of the most important figures in history, expanding his empire to unprecedented sizes.It stretched from the Pacific to the Caspian and Black Seas.The creators of the series are based on a new translation of the anonymous chronicle "The Secret History of the Mongols", which is the oldest known monument of writing of the steppe peoples of Central Asia A fascinating history lesson that combines a story about the past with spectacular stagings like a blockbuster.The six-episode documentary series presents little-known facts from the life of the legendary leader, focusing on his subsequent conquests and centuries-old legacy.The authors based their work on a new translation of the anonymous chronicle "The Secret History of the Mongols", which is the oldest known written monument of the steppe peoples of Central Asia.The experts' statements are the background of a historical thriller in which the realities of the 12th and 13th centuries have been carefully recreated.Thanks to this, the distant past becomes vivid and exciting.A boy from the steppes The starting point of the story about the life of the founder of the Mongol Empire are the turbulent years of his youth.Temujin, later known as Genghis Khan, experienced the decline of his family, poverty and a life of insecurity.He witnessed the tribes fighting.Viewers will see how this survival school influenced his ambitions and character development.As a young warrior, Genghis Khan formed alliances to save his abducted wife, Börte.Thanks to the marriage, he gained the support of the Kereite chief.He consistently made decisions that allowed him to create the largest land empire in history.

credits.actor: Self - French Institute for Central Asian Studies - Marie Favereau
Self - National University of Mongolia - Bayarsaikhan Dashdondog

programme.title: Iron Curtain: Life Under Soviet Occupation
programme.desc: A story about the Sovietization process, from 1944 to the construction of the Berlin Wall.After World War II, the countries of Central and Eastern Europe found themselves in the Soviet zone.The events that led to such a division of the Old Continent will be presented.Viewers will learn how it affected the everyday lives of citizens of these countries and what forms of resistance they encountered.Experts will explain the mechanisms of Soviet domination.There will be witnesses' accounts, interviews, discussions of scientific works and archives including propaganda films, home recordings and fictional stories.

programme.title: Nazi Megastructures: Russia's War
programme.desc: The story of Hitler's attack on Russia - an offensive carried out by the largest military forces ever assembled in history.Documentarians also recall the fierce resistance the Germans encountered during the siege of Sevastopol.This city on the Crimean Peninsula was one of the most heavily defended fortresses in the world.The Nazis launched their second offensive in June 1942.The Germans and Romanians concentrated nine divisions and brought the largest gun in the world - "Dora" with a caliber of 800 mm.A total of over two hundred thousand soldiers attacked the Soviets.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: On April 26, 1986, an explosion destroyed the fourth block of the Chernobyl power plant.The accident was caused by design defects in the reactor and errors during the safety test.It was the worst nuclear disaster in history.The explosion released 400 times more radiation than the bomb dropped on Hiroshima.40 years after the tragedy, its consequences will be shown - from KGB activities to military operations in Ukraine.There will be plenty of witness accounts that will shed new light on the dramatic events.The speakers will include:a liquidator working on the roof of reactor 3, and journalist Wyatt Andrews, who was covering the information blackout in Moscow.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: On April 26, 1986, an explosion destroyed the fourth block of the Chernobyl power plant.The accident was caused by design defects in the reactor and errors during the safety test.It was the worst nuclear disaster in history.The explosion released 400 times more radiation than the bomb dropped on Hiroshima.40 years after the tragedy, its consequences will be shown - from KGB activities to military operations in Ukraine.There will be plenty of witness accounts that will shed new light on the dramatic events.The speakers will include:a liquidator working on the roof of reactor 3, and journalist Wyatt Andrews, who was covering the information blackout in Moscow.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.sub-title: A carnage in the city center
programme.desc: The series' creators solve the mysteries of the biggest plane crashes.Trying to recreate the events that took place before the tragedies, they reconstruct the situations that preceded them on board the machines.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation Special Report 4
programme.desc: Analysis of the most tragic accidents in the history of aviation.The investigation is based on reconstruction of events, photographic materials, accounts of survivors and rescuers, and modern computer graphics.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation 21
programme.desc: Reconstructions of real plane crashes.By recreating the course of events, the program's creators try to understand what led to the unfortunate events.They recreate the circumstances in which the tragedy occurred: the weather, the atmosphere on board the plane, the decisions and actions of the pilots.They also show the work of people investigating the causes of disasters and conduct a computer flight simulation.Viewers can hear various comments from specialists and testimonies of witnesses to these events.The creators of the program will prove that air accidents are often caused by human slowness and lack of imagination.Each disaster is thoroughly analyzed and the lessons learned help minimize the number of tragedies in the future.

programme.title: Seconds from Disaster 6
programme.desc: A reminder of the greatest disasters of our times.The program includes, among others:transport accidents, building collapses and terrorist attacks.

programme.title: Seconds from Disaster 6
programme.desc: A reminder of the greatest disasters of our times.The program includes, among others:transport accidents, building collapses and terrorist attacks.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: The series' creators solve the mysteries of the biggest plane crashes.Trying to recreate the events that took place before the tragedies, they reconstruct the situations that preceded them on board the machines.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of air accidents in recent years.Each episode will present a reconstruction of the events that led to the tragedy.The stories are supplemented by reports from experts and eyewitnesses.

programme.title: Drain the Oceans
programme.desc: The sea depths hide many secrets.The latest technologies will allow you to show wrecks, sunken treasures, cities and even surprising geological formations.One of the puzzles is the sinking of the unfinished aircraft carrier Graf Zeppelin.Scientists will also embark on a journey across the Pacific Ocean to explore the Pacific Ring of Fire - a region of frequent earthquakes and volcanic eruptions.Additionally, specialists will analyze the history of wars, piracy and slavery in the Gulf of Mexico region and the collapse of the largest ancient civilizations in the Mediterranean basin.

credits.actor: Self - Narrator: National Geographic Channel - Russell Boulter
Self - Norwegian University of Science and Technology - Fredrik Soreide
Self - Author and Historian - Craig L. Symonds

programme.title: The Infinite Explorer with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Professor Hannah Fry sets off on a journey around the world.He will visit Spain, Vietnam, Greece, South Korea, Ireland and Iceland.You will admire stunning landscapes and bustling cities.

programme.title: Inside Airport Lost \u0026 Found
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the work of the lost and found office.Search teams make every effort to return the most surprising lost items to travelers.These include artificial eyes, luxury watches and ritual robes.

programme.title: Toronto Airport Uncovered
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the operation of the largest airport in Canada.Viewers will see how the staff ensures traffic flows during record snowfall and negative temperatures.

programme.title: Iceland: Cold War Frontier
programme.desc: The fact that foreign troops were stationed in Iceland for 55 years as part of their obligations to the North Atlantic Alliance had a significant impact on the culture, politics and economy of the island.

programme.title: To Catch A Smuggler 5
programme.desc: It is estimated that 124 million people fly to the United States every year.Among them are smugglers and criminals.Employees of the US Department of Homeland Security are tracking them and protecting the border.

programme.title: Border Protection Spain
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the work of units guarding the Spanish coast.Highly trained professionals have modern equipment.They fight drug smuggling and illegal emigration.

credits.actor: Self - Biologist at Palma Aquarium Foundation - Xisca

programme.title: Genghis Khan: the Secret History of the Mongols
programme.desc: A profile of a man who went down in history as a brilliant military strategist.The six-episode series will present little-known facts from the life of the fearless leader.The authors will show how, after a difficult childhood and captivity, he managed to gain power, unite the quarreling tribes, build an invincible army and subjugate all those who opposed him, including his former allies.Impressive conquests will be discussed, including:expansion into the West, campaigns against the neighboring Tangut people and the Chinese Jin Empire.The troops he commanded were known for the mobility of their cavalry, carrying out rapid attacks and retreats, and applying psychological pressure on their opponents.They used innovative techniques when besieging even well-fortified cities.Genghis Khan became one of the most important figures in history, expanding his empire to unprecedented sizes.It stretched from the Pacific to the Caspian and Black Seas.The creators of the series are based on a new translation of the anonymous chronicle "The Secret History of the Mongols", which is the oldest known written monument of the steppe peoples of Central Asia.

credits.actor: Self - French Institute for Central Asian Studies - Marie Favereau
Self - National University of Mongolia - Bayarsaikhan Dashdondog

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: On April 26, 1986, an explosion destroyed the fourth block of the Chernobyl power plant.The accident was caused by design defects in the reactor and errors during the safety test.It was the worst nuclear disaster in history.The explosion released 400 times more radiation than the bomb dropped on Hiroshima.40 years after the tragedy, its consequences will be shown - from KGB activities to military operations in Ukraine.There will be plenty of witness accounts that will shed new light on the dramatic events.The speakers will include:liquidator working on the roof of reactor 3 and journalist Wyatt Andrews, who was covering the information blockade in Moscow Reconstruction of the first day after the failure.On April 26, 1986, an explosion destroyed the fourth block of the Chernobyl power plant.This is the worst nuclear disaster in history.The explosion released 400 times more radiation than the bomb dropped on Hiroshima.Facility staff and firefighters are trying to deal with the consequences of the accident.The midwifery authorities a few kilometers away in Pripyat tell the residents to function normally.Lessons take place at school and weddings take place at the office.Meanwhile, a radioactive cloud spreads over Europe.

programme.title: Nazi Megastructures: Russia's War
programme.desc: One of Hitler's aspirations was to liquidate the Russian nation in order to gain living space for the Aryans.The German army reached the outskirts of Moscow, but the Red Army managed to stop the attack.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: While landing in Düsseldorf, the turboprop flying Nürnberger Flugdienst 108 crashes. Everyone on board is killed.As part of the investigation, elements of the wreck from the disaster area should be secured.The experts face a real challenge - they must reconstruct the entire machine to determine the causes of the tragedy.The discovery of evidence of a lightning strike is a breakthrough in the case.It turns out that the discharge damaged the electrical system, which resulted in a loss of power.
